title: 10 best herbal teas for heartburn
intro_desc: Herbal teas for heartburn are infusions made from various plants and herbs, specially formulated to alleviate the symptoms of heartburn. These teas provide a natural and gentle alternative to over-the-counter medication, offering those who suffer from heartburn a more soothing and holistic method of managing their discomfort. By sipping on these teas, the muscles of the esophagus can be relaxed, reducing the constriction that often triggers heartburn. Furthermore, herbal teas can help increase gastric juices' alkalinity, neutralizing stomach acids and creating a less acidic environment within the stomach. As a result, the risk of acid reflux is diminished, and the individual can enjoy improved digestion and a decrease in heartburn symptoms. With regular consumption, herbal teas for heartburn empower those struggling with this condition to reclaim their daily lives, free from the pain and irritation that once held them back.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tea helps with heartburn because it contains antispasmodic properties that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating the symptoms of heartburn. The anti-inflammatory properties of chamomile tea are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, particularly apigenin, which helps to relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce the production of stomach acid. By doing so, chamomile tea promotes proper digestion and reduces the likelihood of acid reflux, offering relief to those who suffer from heartburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile tea can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, helping to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with heartburn, due to the presence of volatile oils such as alpha-bisabolol and matricin.
Spasmolytic: Chamomile tea has the ability to relax smooth muscles, which can help prevent the spasms that can cause or worsen heartburn, due to its flavonoids content.
Mucilage: Chamomile tea forms a soothing protective layer on the lining of the esophagus, helping to prevent acid reflux, due to its high mucilage content.
Antacid: Chamomile tea has a natural antacid effect which neutralizes stomach acid, reducing the symptoms of heartburn, due to its tannin content.
Carminative: Chamomile tea promotes the expulsion of gas from the stomach and intestines, preventing the formation of acid and the occurrence of heartburn, due to its volatile oils.

Anxiolytic: Chamomile tea can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, helping to prevent the onset of heartburn, due to its flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin, acting as benzodiazepine receptor-binding agents.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain volatile oils and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and relieve heartburn.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and tannins that can help soothe irritation, reduce inflammation, and alleviate the symptoms of heartburn.
Rhyzomes: Contain high levels of phenolic compounds that can help heal and protect the mucous membrane of the esophagus, reducing the severity of heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers.
Boil four cups of water in a saucepan for five minutes.
Steep chamomile flowers in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove flowers.
Drink warm tea slowly, up to three times a day after meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with heartburn because it contains compounds called glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining caused by acid reflux. Glycyrrhizin also stimulates the production of mucus, which protects the gastrointestinal tract from acid and bile. Additionally, licorice tea increases the mucosal cell growth in the gastrointestinal tract, providing a barrier against acid and bile. Furthermore, licorice tea has been shown to increase the secretion of gastric mucus, which helps to neutralize stomach acid and protect the stomach lining. Overall, licorice tea's anti-inflammatory, mucosal-protective, and acid-neutralizing effects make it an effective natural remedy for heartburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn, due to the presence of glycyrrhizic and glycyrrhetic acids.
Mucosal protective: The demulcent properties of licorice tea can protect the mucous membrane of the esophagus from acid damage, thus reducing heartburn, owing to its polysaccharide content.

Gastric mucosal cell regeneration: Licorice tea stimulates the regeneration of stomach and esophageal cells, promoting healing and preventing heartburn, as a result of its flavonoid and triterpenoid constituents.
Acid reflux prevention: Licorice tea can help maintain the sphincter between the esophagus and stomach, preventing acid reflux, due to the antacid effects of glycyrrhizic acid.
Anti-secretory: Licorice tea can reduce gastric acid secretion, alleviating heartburn, attributed to the presence of compounds like liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin.

Antimicrobial: Licorice tea exhibits antimicrobial properties, eliminating harmful bacteria like H. pylori, which can contribute to acid reflux and heartburn, thanks to the active constituents, such as flavonoids and coumarins.
Antispasmodic: Licorice tea may help relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and relieving symptoms of heartburn, owing to the presence of glycyrrhizin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, which soothes the stomach lining and aids in reducing heartburn.
Rhizomes: Contain compounds that inhibit gastric acid secretion, providing heartburn relief.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, reducing inflammation in the esophagus and alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Flowers: Packed with flavonoids known to reduce gastric acid production, thus preventing heartburn.
Seeds: Contain essential oils that help relax the gastrointestinal tract, providing comfort during heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water to a rolling temperature of 212°F for 1-2 minutes.
Add one tablespoon of dried licorice root to the boiling water, then reduce heat to a simmer.
Let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes to extract licorice's healing properties.
Strain the tea to remove licorice root solids, then let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to coat your throat and esophagus for heartburn relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with heartburn because it contains a substance called mucilage, which becomes slippery and gel-like when mixed with water. This mucilage coats the lining of the esophagus, providing a soothing and protective barrier against stomach acid, which can help reduce the symptoms of heartburn. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, further alleviating heartburn symptoms. Furthermore, marshmallow tea has been shown to increase the mucus production in the stomach, which can help protect the stomach lining from irritation and damage caused by stomach acid, providing additional relief from heartburn.
remedy_properties: Demulcent: Marshmallow tea is packed with mucilage, a slippery substance that coats the mouth, throat, and stomach, protecting the mucous membranes and soothing irritation, which is crucial for heartburn relief.
Anti-inflammatory: It contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, helping reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviating heartburn symptoms.

Mucilage content: The high mucilage content in marshmallow tea can help create a protective barrier against stomach acid, preventing direct contact with the esophagus and lessening the severity of heartburn.
Acid neutralization: Marshmallow root has been found to have an acid-neutralizing effect, further counteracting the acidity that contributes to heartburn.

Gastroprotective: Studies suggest that marshmallow has gastroprotective properties that can help safeguard the stomach lining and promote healing, offering a more long-term solution for those who suffer from chronic heartburn. (Active constituents include ascorbic acid, flavonoids, and tannins.)
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain flavonoids that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, providing relief from heartburn.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants that help soothe irritation in the digestive tract and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Roots: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can help heal damage to the esophagus and stomach lining caused by acid reflux.
Stems: Contain tannins that help reduce acid production and create a protective barrier in the stomach, preventing heartburn.
Seeds: High in mucilage, which forms a soothing gel that coats the esophagus and stomach lining, providing instant relief from heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers, letting it steep for 10 minutes.
Stir in 1 tablespoon of honey until fully dissolved.
Mix in 2 crushed marshmallows, stirring continuously until they completely dissolve.
Strain the tea to remove chamomile flowers.
Serve immediately, sipping slowly to soothe heartburn.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tea helps with heartburn because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery and thick when mixed with water. This mucilage coats the lining of the esophagus, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid that causes heartburn. Additionally, slippery elm tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, further alleviating the symptoms of heartburn. Furthermore, the tea can stimulate the production of saliva, which helps to neutralize stomach acid and wash it away from the esophagus, providing additional relief from heartburn.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Slippery elm tea forms a protective barrier over the lining of the esophagus, reducing irritation caused by acid reflux. This is due to the presence of mucilage, a gel-like substance that coats and soothes mucous membranes.

Antacid property: Slippery elm tea helps neutralize stomach acid, which contributes to heartburn symptoms. This is attributed to the alkaline nature of slippery elm, which can counteract acidity in the stomach.

Mucilage production: Slippery elm stimulates the production of mucus, which helps to protect and lubricate the digestive tract, easing the discomfort caused by heartburn. The increased mucus production is a result of the tea's high mucilage content.

Inflammation reduction: Slippery elm tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating heartburn symptoms. This is thanks to the presence of several compounds, including tannins and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects.

Gastric motility improvement: Slippery elm tea may help improve gastric motility, allowing for more efficient digestion and reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. This is believed to be due to the fiber content in slippery elm, which can help promote healthy digestion and gut motility.
remedy_parts: Bark: Contains substantial amounts of mucilage, making it an effective coating for the lining of the digestive tract and providing heartburn relief.
Leaves: Rich in tannins that contribute astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe irritated gastrointestinal tissues.
Inner Bark: High in antioxidants that can protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation, providing heartburn symptom alleviation.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Pour 2 cups of water into a saucepan.
Obtain - Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried slippery elm bark.
Mix - Stir the bark into the water and bring to a boil.
Simmer - Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain - Remove from heat, strain, then drink the tea while warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Meadowsweet tea helps with heartburn because it contains high levels of tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus. Additionally, meadowsweet tea includes a compound called salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory that can help relieve the pain and discomfort associated with heartburn. Furthermore, meadowsweet tea has been historically used as a digestive aid, promoting the production of mucus in the stomach lining, which can protect it from acid reflux, thus further alleviating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Meadowsweet tea contains salicylic acid, which helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn.
Astringent: The tannins present in meadowsweet tea have an astringent effect, helping to decrease irritation and soothe mucous membranes affected by heartburn.
Mucilaginous: Meadowsweet tea contains mucilage, which forms a protective layer on the stomach lining, preventing acid reflux and alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Antacid: The flavonoids in meadowsweet tea help neutralize stomach acid, reducing the severity of heartburn and its associated discomfort.
Digestive aid: Meadowsweet tea stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, improving overall digestion and helping to prevent heartburn caused by poor digestion.
McGuffin M, Hobbs C, Upton R, Goldberg A, editors. American Herbal Products Association's Botanical Safety Handbook, 2nd edition. Boca Raton: CRC Press; 2016.
Wichtl M. Herbal Drugs and Phytopharmaceuticals: A Handbook for Practice on a Scientific Basis. 5th ed. Stuttgart
European Scientific Cooperative on Phytotherapy.: ESCOP; 1997.
Blumenthal M, Goldberg A, Brinckmann J. Herbal Medicine: Expanded Commission E Monographs. Newton
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain high amounts of tannins that help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system, providing relief for heartburn.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, offering antioxidant properties that protect the gut lining and alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Stems: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds and essential oils which help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, thus preventing acid reflux and heartburn.
Rhizomes: Packed with salicylic acid, providing pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects, which is beneficial for treating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried meadowsweet flowers and place in a tea infuser.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour over the infuser in a teapot or cup.
Steep the meadowsweet for 5-10 minutes, depending on desired strength.
Remove the infuser and discard or compost the spent flowers.
Drink the tea warm, up to three times a day, to relieve heartburn symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Catnip tea helps with heartburn because it contains antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the gastrointestinal system, reducing discomfort and pain associated with heartburn. Its calming effect on the stomach lining helps prevent acid reflux, while its natural antacid properties neutralize stomach acid, making it an effective natural remedy for heartburn relief. Moreover, catnip tea stimulates the production of digestive juices, facilitating proper digestion, and preventing the onset of heartburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Catnip tea helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms, due to its active constituents such as nepetalactone and volatile oils.
Spasmolytic: It helps relax the gastrointestinal muscles, alleviating spasms and cramps that contribute to heartburn, by containing compounds like nepetalactone and flavonoids.
Carminative: Catnip tea promotes the elimination of excess gas from the stomach, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with heartburn, due to its constituents like volatile oils and terpenoids.

Diuretic: It assists in eliminating fluids from the body, which helps decrease pressure within the digestive system, minimizing the risk of acid reflux and heartburn, thanks to its active ingredients like nepetin, acids, and tannins.

Mild sedative: Catnip tea may help reduce stress and anxiety, which can lead to heartburn, as it contains volatile oils and antioxidants that promote relaxation, improving digestion and overall gastrointestinal health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain oils and acids that aid in digestion and easing heartburn symptoms.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe irritated stomach lining.
Stems: Rich in antioxidants that support overall digestive health and reduce heartburn.
Seeds: Packed with nutrients and oils, they help protect the stomach lining and alleviate heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried catnip leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the catnip leaves in the boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the catnip leaves.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Sip the tea slowly to soothe heartburn.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow dock tea helps with heartburn because it contains high amounts of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory, and antihistamine compounds. These properties work together to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, soothe digestion, and promote the production of mucus in the gastrointestinal tract. By creating a protective barrier, yellow dock tea helps prevent the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus, effectively alleviating heartburn symptoms. Additionally, yellow dock tea stimulates bile production, which aids in the breakdown and absorption of fats, further contributing to a healthy digestive process and minimizing the likelihood of heartburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yellow dock tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of anthraquinones, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by heartburn.
Astringent: The astringent properties of yellow dock, from tannins, help shrink and constrict inflamed tissues, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.

Alterative: Yellow dock's alterative properties, due to the presence of calcium, iron, manganese, and phosphorus, promote the body's own detoxification processes, which can help prevent heartburn by maintaining a healthy digestive system.

Mild laxative: The mild laxative properties of yellow dock, due to anthraquinones such as emodin, can help regulate bowel movements, preventing strain on the stomach and reducing the likelihood of heartburn.
Demulcent: Yellow dock tea's demulcent properties, derived from mucilage, create a soothing and protective coating on the stomach lining, alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Choleretic: Yellow dock's choleretic properties, due to its alkaloids, stimulate the production of bile, promoting better digestion and reducing the risk of heartburn.
Antacid: Yellow dock tea's antacid properties can neutralize stomach acid, offering immediate relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in compounds that reduce inflammation, soothing irritation in the digestive tract and alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to heal stomach lining and prevent heartburn.
Flowers: Packed with tannins that help decrease acidity in the stomach, providing relief from heartburn.
Seeds: High in fiber, aiding in digestion, reducing acid production, and preventing heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried yellow dock root and 1 cup of fresh water.
Pour the water into a saucepan and bring it to a boil.
Add the yellow dock root to the boiling water, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solid plant material.
Drink the tea while still warm, up to three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with heartburn because it contains gingerol, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus. This can alleviate the symptoms of heartburn by preventing the backup of stomach acid into the esophagus. Additionally, ginger tea stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, which can help speed up the digestion process and reduce the amount of acid in the stomach. Furthermore, ginger tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help prevent the symptoms of heartburn caused by anxiety or stress.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger tea can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, helping to alleviate the symptoms of heartburn by decreasing irritation and promoting healing. This property is due to the presence of gingerols and shogaols.

Gastric motility enhancement: Ginger tea can help speed up the emptying of the stomach, which may prevent the backflow of stomach acid and reduce heartburn symptoms. This is attributed to gingerols and shogaols as well.

Antiemetic: Ginger tea has been shown to help alleviate nausea and vomiting, which can be associated with heartburn or gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). This is attributed mainly to 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol.

Mucoprotective: Ginger tea has a protective effect on the gastric mucosa, helping to shield the stomach lining from acid and thereby reducing the likelihood of heartburn. The mucosal protective effect is attributed to the presence of gingerols.

Antioxidant: Ginger tea contains antioxidant compounds that can help reduce oxidative stress in the body, contributing to overall gut health and potentially alleviating heartburn symptoms. The primary antioxidant compounds in ginger are gingerols, shogaols, and paradols.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are widely used due to their natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing heartburn relief.
Leaves: Ginger leaves contain beneficial compounds that help relax and soothe gastrointestinal muscles, alleviating symptoms of heartburn.
Flowers: Ginger flowers have strong antioxidant properties that help protect the digestive system from damage, reducing the risk of heartburn incidents.
Seeds: Ginger seeds are rich in essential oils that stimulate digestion, helping to prevent the onset of heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Peel and grate 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger root.
Boil 2 cups of water, then add grated ginger, reduce heat, and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the ginger-infused water into a cup, discarding the solid ginger.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice.
Drink the ginger tea while warm, up to three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tea helps with heartburn because it contains natural compounds that have a calming effect on the digestive system. These compounds, known as valepotriates, work by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of heartburn. Additionally, valerian tea is known to have muscle-relaxing properties, which can help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from backing up into the esophagus. Overall, valerian tea provides a natural and effective way to soothe heartburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Valerian tea can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn, as it contains volatile oils and alkaloids.
Spasmolytic: Valerian tea can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing muscle spasms that contribute to heartburn, because it contains valepotriates and valerenic acid.
Anxiolytic: Valerian tea has calming effects that can help alleviate stress-induced heartburn, as it contains valerenic acid and valepotriates.
Carminative: Valerian tea can help expel gas from the digestive tract and relieve bloating, indirectly addressing one of the causes of heartburn, due to its volatile oils content.
Sedative: Valerian tea can promote better sleep, which in turn may help manage gastroesophageal reflux, as it contains valerenic acid and valepotriates.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants, the roots of valerian tea plant help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing heartburn relief.
Rhizomes: Containing volatile oils, valerian rhizomes help alleviate symptoms of heartburn by promoting relaxation and reducing acid reflux.
Leaves: Valerian leaves, high in flavonoids, offer anti-spasmodic properties to soothe and prevent heartburn.
Flowers: With calming effects, valerian flowers offer relief from heartburn by reducing stress-induced acid production.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried valerian root with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, covered, to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea to remove the valerian root.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your heartburn.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tea helps with heartburn because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate the symptoms of acid reflux, which is a common cause of heartburn. The tea's soothing effect on the gastrointestinal system can reduce the production of stomach acid, providing relief from the burning sensation in the chest and throat. Skullcap is also known to have muscle relaxation properties, which can help the esophagus muscles to relax, preventing stomach acid from backing up into the esophagus. Regular consumption of skullcap tea can therefore offer a natural and effective remedy for heartburn.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Skullcap tea can reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with heartburn. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antispasmodic: Skullcap tea can help relax the smooth muscles of the esophagus, reducing spasms and the sensation of heartburn. This is attributed to the presence of triterpene glycosides and volatile oils.

Anxiolytic: Skullcap tea can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help prevent heartburn caused by stress and anxiety-related behaviors (such as overeating or lying down after eating). This is due to the presence of flavonoids, volatile oils, and bitter compounds.

Anti-secretory: Skullcap tea can help reduce the production of stomach acid, which can help prevent heartburn caused by acid reflux. This property is due to the presence of flavonoids, volatile oils, and tannins.

Antioxidant: Skullcap tea can help protect the lining of the esophagus from damage caused by stomach acid, which can help prevent heartburn. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive tract, providing relief for heartburn.
Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Buds: Packed with essential oils, they help stimulate digestion, relax the gastrointestinal tract, and provide soothing relief for heartburn.
Stems: Contain beneficial compounds that promote healthy digestion and help reduce discomfort caused by heartburn.
Seeds: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants, they aid in digestion and help alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle for 3-5 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of dried skullcap herb to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the boiled water over the skullcap herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent herb.
Drink the skullcap tea while warm, up to three times a day, for heartburn relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat heartburn with herbal teas is by choosing ones with natural antacid and anti-inflammatory properties. Chamomile, ginger, and licorice root teas are excellent options. Chamomile helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus, while ginger aids in digestion and neutralizing stomach acid. Licorice root tea coats the esophagus and stomach lining, providing a protective barrier against acid. Drink these teas after meals and before bedtime for best results, but avoid drinking them with meals, as this can dilute stomach acids necessary for digestion.
supplementary_causes: The primary causes of heartburn that are treatable with herbal teas are overeating, eating too quickly, and consuming spicy, fatty, or greasy foods. Additionally, heartburn can be triggered by lying down after eating, wearing tight clothing, and experiencing emotional stress or anxiety. Herbal teas can help alleviate these symptoms by reducing inflammation, soothing the digestive tract, and promoting the flow of digestive juices.
supplementary_preparations: Other herbal preparations to use with teas for heartburn are tinctures, glycerites, and infused oils. These preparations allow for a variety of ways to consume heartburn-relieving herbs, including sublingually, topically, and in capsule form. Additionally, there are herbal lozenges, syrups, and compresses, offering further relief and customization for individual needs. These diverse methods ensure a comprehensive herbal approach to managing heartburn, enhancing the effectiveness of the tea remedy.
supplementary_frequency: For heartburn, you should drink herbal teas up to three times a day. This frequency aids in neutralizing stomach acid and reducing inflammation, as the natural compounds found in these teas can help soothe the digestive tract. However, it's important to avoid drinking herbal teas excessively, as this might lead to disrupting your regular eating and drinking patterns, potentially worsening heartburn symptoms.
supplementary_side_effects: The possible side effects associated with consuming herbal teas for heartburn are stomach discomfort, nausea, and diarrhea. Some herbal teas can interact with medications, so it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before consuming them. Additionally, excessive intake of certain herbal teas, like peppermint or chamomile, can worsen heartburn symptoms in some individuals. It's crucial to drink these teas in moderation and be mindful of any adverse reactions.
